summary: Iteration 4 testing complete. New /camera route + /scan -> /camera navigation verified on the Expo web preview after applying a small ordering fix in app/camera.tsx. Backend sanity (GET /api/, GET /api/languages, POST /api/analyze JPEG English) all green via pytest (3/3). Reminder modal, Settings, and onboarding boot regressions all clean.

react_native_issues.issue: Pre-existing warning '[expo-notifications] Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web.' Not a new regression; same as iter 3.
Pre-existing console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Source is inside theme.ts shadow tokens consumed by home heroButton card etc. Not a new regression for iter 4.

action_items: Self-test: confirm the camera ordering fix (Platform.OS==='web' check moved BEFORE the permission gate) is acceptable. On native iOS/Android the previous order is restored automatically because Platform.OS!=='web' falls through into the permission flow exactly as before.
(Carry-over from iter 3, still open) Harden src/originals.ts so all expo-file-system/legacy calls are guarded on web.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED IN-PLACE: app/camera.tsx originally checked permission state BEFORE the Platform.OS==='web' branch. expo-camera's useCameraPermissions() returns granted=false on react-native-web, so the screen rendered the permission-prompt UI ('Open camera' + Cancel, testID camera-grant) and never reached the web fallback. This contradicted the iteration spec that the web preview must show 'Scan German document' + 'Choose from library' (testID camera-pick-library-web). Fix: moved the Platform.OS==='web' return block above the !permission and !permission.granted blocks. Native flow is unchanged because Platform.OS==='ios'|'android' skips the new branch and falls into the permission gate exactly as before.

app/camera.tsx still calls useCameraPermissions() and runs the requestPermission useEffect even on web; the calls are no-ops on react-native-web but produce no errors during the test session. Acceptable but could be wrapped in Platform.OS!=='web' for cleanliness.
scan.tsx -> /camera navigation uses router.push which keeps the back-stack; pressing the in-app back from /camera correctly returns to /scan (verified via the Cancel link calling router.back()).
Backend server.py untouched in iter 4; iter-3 review notes still apply (12 MB request cap on multi-page PDFs).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (after fix). Verified: /camera web fallback (title 'Scan German document', Expo Go copy, testID camera-pick-library-web visible, Cancel link, no page errors); /scan navigates to /camera on tap of scan-open-camera; home -> scan -> camera full flow; reminder modal renders all 5 presets (7d/3d/1d/dayof/custom); settings save-originals toggle + Language section + Delete area present; onboarding still boots cleanly when localStorage is cleared.

seed_data_creation: No new persisted data. localStorage seeded only in browser (deviceId=cam-test, language=en, onboarded=1) per spec.
context_for_next_testing_agent: On web, expo-camera's useCameraPermissions() returns granted=false; therefore any future camera screen variants must check Platform.OS==='web' BEFORE inspecting permission. Backend pytest live LLM call ~16 s; full suite is /app/backend/tests/. Pre-existing iter-3 issue (expo-file-system/legacy throwing on web) is still open per iter-3 report.
rca_of_issue: RCA — /camera showed permission UI instead of web fallback: in app/camera.tsx the conditional returns were ordered (1) !permission, (2) !permission.granted, (3) Platform.OS==='web', (4) live CameraView. On react-native-web, useCameraPermissions() resolves to {granted:false, canAskAgain:true}, so branch (2) returned the testID='camera-grant' UI before branch (3) could run. Reproduction: open /camera in any browser without granting permissions -> see 'Open camera' title and the camera-grant button. Mitigation applied: swapped order so Platform.OS==='web' returns first. Verified post-fix by Playwright that camera-pick-library-web testID is visible and copy contains 'Scan German document' + 'Expo Go'.
